title: Circuit breaker with multi-port bistable element
abstract: A circuit breaker (CB) comprises a fixed contact (1), a moving contact (2), and a bistable element (100). The moving contact (2) is disposed at the bistable element (100). The circuit breaker (CB) further comprises an actuation element (200) to manually switch the moving contact (2) between the open and closed state. The bistable element (100) is configured to be moved by the actuation element (200) such that the bistable element (100) moves to a first stable state in which the moving contact (2) is in the closed state and to a second stable state in which the moving contact (2) is in the open state. The bistable element (100) is configured as a compliant element to be elastically deformed during the movement between the first and second stable state.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A circuit breaker, comprising: - a fixed contact (1), - a moving contact (2), - a bistable element (100), - wherein the moving contact (2) is disposed at the bistable element (100), - wherein the bistable element (100) is configured to move the moving contact (2) in an open and closed state, wherein, in the open state, the moving contact (2) is isolated from the fixed contact (1), and, in the closed state, the moving contact (2) is electrically connected to the fixed contact (1) , - an actuation element (200) to manually switch the moving contact (2) between the open and closed state, - wherein the bistable element (100) is configured to be moved by the actuation element (200) such that the bistable element (100) moves to a first stable state in which the moving contact (2) is in the closed state and to a second stable state in which the moving contact (2) is in the open state, - wherein the bistable element (100) is configured to be deformed during the movement between the first and second stable state. 2. The circuit breaker of claim 1, wherein the bistable element (100) is configured such that energy is stored in the bistable element (100) by the deformation of the bistable element (100), wherein the stored energy is lower in the first and the second stable state of the bistable element (100) than in a state of the bistable element (100) between the the first and second stable state. 3. The circuit breaker of claims 1 or 2, comprising: 

- a supporting element (300), wherein the bistable element (100) is mounted on the supporting element (300), - a pin joint (310) being mounted on the supporting element (300) , - wherein the bistable element (100) has a first end (101) being rotatably arranged at the pin joint (310), - wherein the moving contact (2) is fixed to the first end (101) of the bistable element (100) so that the moving contact (2) is moved between the open and closed state by the rotation of the bistable element (100) around the pin joint (310) . 4. The circuit breaker of claim 3, - wherein the bistable element (100) and the actuation element (200) are configured to be in one of a latched and unlatched configuration, - wherein, in the latched configuration, the actuation element (200) is coupled to the bistable element (100) and, in the unlatched configuration, the actuation element (200) is uncoupled from the bistable element (100) . 5. The circuit breaker of claim 4, - wherein the bistable element (100) comprises a second end (102) , - wherein the supporting element (300) comprises a slot (330) , - wherein the bistable element (100) comprises a guiding pin (110) being arranged at the second end (102) of the bistable element (100), - wherein the guiding pin (110) is guided within the slot (330) when the moving contact (2) is manually switched by the actuation element (200) between the open and closed state. 6. The circuit breaker of claim 5, 

- wherein the bistable element (100) is in the first stable state and the moving contact (2) is in the closed state, when the guiding pin (110) is positioned in the slot (330) at a first position (SI), - wherein the bistable element (100) is in the second stable state and the moving contact (2) is in the open state, when the guiding pin (110) is positioned in the slot (330) at a second position (S2). 7. The circuit breaker of claim 6, wherein the bistable element (100) is configured to be deformed, when the guiding pin (110) is moved in the slot (330) from one of the frist and second position (SI, S2) to a third position (S3) between the first and the second position (SI, S2) . 8. The circuit breaker of claim 7, - wherein the bistable element (100) is configured such that the guiding pin (110) moves by itself in the slot (330) from the third position (S3) to the second position (S2), after the guiding pin (110) is moved by the actuation element (200) from the first position (SI) to the third position (S3) of the slot (330) , - wherein the bistable element (100) is configured such that the guiding pin (110) moves by itself in the slot (330) from the third position (S3) to the first position (SI), after the guiding pin (110) is moved by the actuation element (200) from the second position (S2) to the third position (S3) of the slot (330) . 9. The circuit breaker of claims 5 to 8, - wherein the actuation element (200) comprises a first and a second arm (210, 220), 

- wherein the bistable element (100) comprises an engaging element (120) being arranged at the second end (102) of the bistable element (100), - wherein the bistable element (100) and the actuation element (200) are configured such that, in the latched configuration of the bistable element and the actuation element, the engaging element (120) of the bistable element (100) is arranged in an area between the first and the second arm (210, 220) of the actuation element (200), - wherein the bistable element (100) and the actuation element (200) are configured such that, in the unlatched configuration of the bistable element (100) and the actuation element (200), the engaging element (120) of the bistable element (100) is arranged outside the area of between the first and the second arm (210, 220) of the actuation element (200) . 10. The circuit breaker of claim 9, - wherein the actuation element (200) comprises a knob (230) to manually switch the moving contact (2) between the open and closed state, - wherein the bistable element (100) and the actuation element (200) are configured such that, in the latched configuration of the bistable element (100) and the actuation element (200), the first arm (210) of the actuation element (200) is in contact with the engaging element (120) of the bistable element to move the guiding pin (110) of the bistable element in the slot (330) from the second position (S2) to the third position (S3) to move the moving contact (2) to the closed state, when manually moving the knob (230) in a first direction, - wherein the bistable element (100) and the actuation element (200) are configured such that, in the latched configuration of the bistable element (100) and the actuation element (200), the second arm (220) of the actuation element 

 (200) is in contact with the engaging element (120) of the bistable element to move the guiding pin (110) of the bistable element in the slot (330) from the first position (SI) to the third position (S3) to move the moving contact (2) to the open state, when manually moving the knob (230) in a second direction. 11. The circuit breaker of claims 9 or 10, - wherein the first arm (210) of the actuation element (200) is configured to be flexible, - wherein, when the configuration of the circuit breaker is changed from the unlatched to the latched configuration, the first arm (210) of the actuation element (200) slides over a surface (121) of the engaging element (120) of the bistable element and is bent by the engaging element (120) in a direction vertical to the supporting element (300) and snaps back after slipping off the surface (121) of the engaging element (120) so that the engaging element (120) is arranged in the area between the first and second arm (210, 220) of the actuation element. 12. The circuit breaker of claims 5 to 11, comprising: - a tripping element (400) to trip a movement of the moving contact (2) from the closed to the open state, - wherein the tripping element (400) comprises an arm (410) to exert a force to the tripping element (400), - wherein the tripping element (400) is rotatably arranged on the supporting element (300) such that the tripping element (400) performs a rotational movement, when exerting the force to the tripping element (400), - wherein the tripping element (400) lifts in a direction vertical to the supporting element (300) during the rotational movement of the tripping element (400) . 13. The circuit breaker of claim 12, 

- wherein the bistable element (100) is coupled to the tripping element (400) such that the bistable element (100) lifts in the direction vertical to the supporting element (300) and the guiding pin (110) of the bistable element is moved out of the slot (330) during the rotational movement of the tripping element (400), - wherein the bistable element (100) performs a rotational movement around the pin joint (310) such that the moving contact (2) is moved from the closed to the open state, when the guiding pin (110) of the bistable element (100) is moved out of the slot (330) . 14. The circuit breaker of claims 12 or 13, - wherein the supporting element (300) comprises a protruding portion (340) having an inclined surface (341), - wherein the tripping element (400) has a contact surface (420) that slides over the inclined surface (341) of the protruding portion (340) of the supporting element (300) during the rotational movement of the tripping element (400) so that the bistable element (100) lifts in a direction vertical to the supporting element (300) . 15. The circuit breaker of claims 12 to 14, comprising: - a lifting element (500) to lift the bistable element (100) in the direction vertical to the supporting element (300), - wherein the lifting element (500) has a contact surface (511) that abuts the bistable element (100), - wherein the lifting element (500) is mounted on the tripping element (400) to perform a rotational movement caused due to the rotational movement of the tripping element, whereby the lifting element (500) lifts in the direction vertical to the supporting element (300), - wherein the lifting element (500) lifts the bistable element (100) in the direction vertical to the supporting 

element (300) so that the guiding pin (110) is moved out of the slot (330) during the rotational movement . 16. The circuit breaker of claims 1 or 2, comprising: - a supporting element (300), - a first and a second pin joint (310, 320) being mounted/disposed on the supporting element (300), - wherein the bistable element (100) is flexible, - wherein the first end (101) of the bistable element (100) is rotatably arranged around the first pin joint (310) and the second end (102) of the bistable element (100) is rotatably arranged around the second pin joint (320), - wherein the moving contact (2) is fixed to the first end (101) of the bistable element (100) so that the moving contact (2) is moved between the open and closed state by the rotation of the bistable element (100) around the first pin joint (310) . 17. The circuit breaker of claim 16, wherein the distance between the first and second pin joint (310, 320) is smaller than the length of the bistable element (100) so that, in the first stable state, the bistable element (100) is bent upwards between the first and the second pin joint (310, 320) and, in the second stable state, the bistable element (100) is bent downwards between the first and the second pin joint (310, 320) . 18. The circuit breaker of claims 16 or 17, wherein the actuation element (200) is coupled to a position of the bistable element (100) between the first and the second end of the bistable element (101, 102) . 19. The circuit breaker of claims 16 to 18, wherein the bistable element (100) and the actuation element (200) are coupled to each other such that the bistable 

element (100) may be pushed upwards by the actuation element (200) to move the bistable element (100) in the first stable state and may be pushed downwards by the actuation element (200) to move the bistable element (100) in the second stable state. 20. The circuit breaker of claims 16 to 19, - wherein the bistable element (100) and the actuation element (200) are coupled to each other by a latching mechanism, - wherein the latching mechanism comprises a latching means (600) and a latching portion (240) of the actuation element (200) , - wherein the latching means (600) is mounted on the bistable element (100), - wherein the latching means (600) can be engaged/disengaged from a latching portion (240) of the actuation element (200) during tripping operation. 21. The circuit breaker of claims 1 or 2, comprising: - a rigid arm (10), - wherein the rigid arm (10) is configured to be moved to a first and a second position, - wherein the bistable element (100) is mounted to the rigid arm (10), - wherein the bistable element (100) is configured to be moved in the first stable state and the second stable state by the movement of the rigid arm (10) between the first and the second position. 22. The circuit breaker of claim 21, - wherein the bistable element (100) has at least one compliant arm (130) having a first and second end (131, 132) respectively fixed to a supporting element (300), 

- wherein the at least one compliant arm (130) of the bistable element (100) is coupled to the rigid arm (10) at a coupling position of the at least one compliant arm (130) of the bistable element, wherein the couling position is located between the first and the second end (131, 132) of the at least one compliant arm (130) of the bistable element (100) . 23. The circuit breaker of claim 21, - wherein the first end (131) of the at least one compliant arm (130) of the bistable element (100) is fixed to at least a first fixing point (351) of the supporting element (300), - wherein the second end (132) of the at least one compliant arm (130) of the bistable element (100) is fixed to at least a second fixing point (352) of the supporting element, - wherein the distance between the at least one first and second fixing point (351, 352) of the supporting element (300) is smaller than the length of the at least one compliant arm (130) so that the at least one compliant arm (130) is bent upwards, when the rigid arm (10) is moved to the first position, and the at least one compliant arm (130) is bent downwards, when the rigid arm (10) is moved to the second position. 24. The circuit breaker of claims 21 to 23, - wherein the actuation element (200) and the rigid arm (10) are configured to be in one of a latched and unlatched configuration, - wherein, in the latched configuration, the actuation element (200) is coupled to the rigid arm (10) to move the rigid arm between the first and second position, - wherein, in the unlatched configuration, the actuation element (200) is uncoupled from the rigid arm (10) . 25. The circuit breaker of claims 24 

wherein the actuation element (200) comprises at least one bearing element (250) being arranged at a first side (201) of the actuation element (200), the at least one bearing element (250) is in contact with a contact face (12) of the rigid arm (10), when the actuation element (200) and the rigid arm (10) are in the latched configuration. 26. The circuit breaker of claim 25, - wherein the rigid arm (10) comprises at least one latching element ( 13 ) , - wherein the actuation element (200) comprises a latching portion (260) , - wherein the at least one latching element (13) of the rigid arm (10) is configured to be engaged in the latching portion (260) of the actuation element (200), when the actuation element (200) and the rigid arm (10) are in the latched configuration . 27. The circuit breaker of claim 26, wherein the at least one latching element (13) is configured as a compliant structure that is bend to be engaged in/disengaged from the latching portion (260) of the actuation element (200). 28. The circuit breaker of claims 25 to 27, - wherein the actuation element (200) comprises a fixing portion (270) at a second side (202) of the actuation element (200), wherein the actuation element (200) is fixed to the supporting element (300) at the fixing portion (270), - wherein the actuation element (200) comprises a spring element (280) being arranged between the fixing portion (270) and the bearing element (250) . 29. The circuit breaker of claims 21 to 28, comprising: 

a tripping element (400) having a first branch (430) being coupled to the bistable element (100), a second and third branch (440, 450) formed to push against the first and second latching element (13) to bend the latching element during engaging with/disengaging from the latching portion (260) of the actuation element (200) .
